items.title: Consumer Tech News (Jan 20-Jan 24): Trump Revokes Biden's AI Policies, Meta Launches New 'Edits' App To Lure TikTok Users & More
items.author: Lekha Gupta
items.description: President Trump revoked Biden's AI executive order, Electronic Arts reported a revenue miss, Meta launched a new video app to compete with TikTok, and other tech news.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Electronic Arts reported a revenue miss for Q3, citing a slowdown in Global Football.
Meta launched a new video creation app called 'Edits' to attract TikTok users.
Tesla bull Ross Gerber voiced doubts about the effectiveness of an executive order in overriding existing laws concerning the TikTok ban.
LinkedIn, owned by Microsoft, has been accused of sharing private data for AI model training without user consent.
Apple is reportedly bringing its AI capabilities to the Mac's Mail app with the upcoming macOS 15.4 update.
Samsung and Google have decided to collaborate for developing augmented reality glasses.
Samsung and Google have decided to collaborate for developing augmented reality glasses.

items.title: 2 ETFs to Buy in 2025 and Hold Forever
items.author: Jennifer Saibil
items.description: The article discusses the benefits of investing in index funds, particularly the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF and the Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF. It highlights the diversification and low-cost advantages of these ETFs, as well as their strong performance over time.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF as a great choice for investors, citing its low expense ratio, diversification, and strong performance as reasons to invest in it.

The article also recommends the Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF, noting its outperformance of the regular S&P 500 ETF and its focus on growth stocks, which can provide additional upside potential for investors.
The article mentions Apple as one of the large, established companies that are included in the Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF, indicating that it is a stable and well-performing stock.
Similar to Apple, the article mentions Microsoft as one of the large, established companies in the Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF, suggesting that it is a strong and reliable investment.
The article also includes Nvidia as one of the large, established companies in the Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF, indicating that it is a high-performing growth stock.
